Apple's next CEO John Ternus tells employees what Apple won't do with AI
description: Days after being named Apple's next CEO, John Ternus walked into a town hall and told employees what Apple won't do with AI: ship technology for technology's sake. With Siri still broken after two years of missed promises, Ternus laid out a two-part AI philosophy—one focused on internal data, the other on products that actually work. The question is whether Apple's restrained approach wins or falls further behind.

China to Mark Zuckerberg's Meta: You cannot buy this company
description: China has blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, citing national security concerns and potential technology leakage. This move follows reports of Beijing restricting American capital in its AI sector. The decision stems from Manus's "general AI agent" technology, developed in China but moved to Singapore, raising fears of intellectual property transfer to US firms.

'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 40: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Pushpa 2’s all-time domestic record
description: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun, is on the verge of becoming the second highest-grossing Indian film of all time globally, with a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,777.52 crore, just Rs. 10.54 crore away from surpassing 'Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's Rs. 1,788.06 crore record. On Day 40, the film's India net stands at Rs. 1,130.69 crore, approximately Rs. 103 crore away from 'Pushpa 2: The Rule's all-time domestic record of Rs. 1,234.1 crore.

Food poisoning due to watermelon? Inside the deaths 4 family members in Mumbai
description: A family of four, including parents and their two daughters, tragically died from suspected food poisoning on Sunday. After a family dinner, they consumed watermelon in the early hours, leading to severe vomiting and diarrhea. Despite medical attention, all four succumbed to the illness, prompting a police investigation.

Neymar left out of World Cup album, sparks exit speculation
description: Neymar’s absence from the 2026 World Cup Panini album highlights his uncertain future with Brazil. After a year-long international absence and a series of injuries, including a knee rupture in 2023, Neymar’s inclusion became uncertain. The album’s selection process, based on recent call-ups and player form, reflects his current situation, underscoring the reality of his race against time for the World Cup.

title: Fact check: Clavicular pregnancy rumour after 10-day romance debunked
description: Clavicular, whose real name is Braden Peters, went viral after claims he is expecting a child with a girlfriend of 10 days. The rumor came from a viral post and a misunderstood livestream. There is no proof of pregnancy. The story is false, while real concerns remain about his health, recent hospital stay, and ongoing controversies.

title: Delhi heatwave threat grows: Govt identifies deadly hotspots; Safdarjung, Ayanagar in red zone
description: Delhi government has identified thermal hotspots using satellite data to combat extreme heat, with areas like Ayanagar and Najafgarh showing high temperatures. The Heat Wave Action Plan 2026 prioritizes targeted interventions for vulnerable populations, including children, outdoor workers, and animals, with measures like increased ORS stock and water availability.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
description: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.
